statement: If there exists an orthonormal basis consisting of eigenvectors of `T`, then `T` is normal
lean_statement:
theorem isStarNormal_of_orthonormal_eigenbasis
    {𝕜 E ι : Type*} [RCLike 𝕜] [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E] [Fintype ι]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →L[𝕜] E)
    (b : OrthonormalBasis ι 𝕜 E)
    (hb : ∀ i, ∃ μ : 𝕜, T (b i) = μ • b i) :
    IsStarNormal T
proof:
(i.e. it commutes with its adjoint, formalized as `IsStarNormal T`).

Proof: choose an orthonormal basis `b` and scalars `μ i` with `T (b i) = μ i • b i`. In this basis,
`T` is diagonal: it sends each basis vector to a scalar multiple of itself. Using the defining
property of the adjoint and orthonormality, one checks that `T† (b i) = conj (μ i) • b i`, so `T†`
is also diagonal in the same basis. Two diagonal operators (equivalently, diagonal matrices) commute,
so `T ∘ T† = T† ∘ T`. Hence `T` is normal.
